verbose_sections.notes: Tutorial content: lists supported dev types, script vs agent heuristics, settings.json permission sample blocks, todo-script boilerplate. Codex finding: section contains some real procedural rules (scope decision, ask-permission contract, todo-script requirement) mixed with verbose example/rationale blocks. Removable sub-blocks: over-engineering example (lines 1313-1329, 17 lines), Why-ask-permission rationale (lines 1350-1356, 7 lines), todo-script sample code (lines 1368-1390, 23 lines). Total removable within section: ~47 lines.

Contains incident narrative (Why this rule exists: lines 1455-1458), two worked examples (WHAT-only correct: 1477-1482, HOW-prescription forbidden: 1483-1488), and expanded scope exclusion list. The rule itself (lines 1459-1476) and enforcement hook pointer are substantive. Removable: incident narrative (~4 lines), worked examples (~12 lines), verbose scope exclusion list (~10 lines). Total removable: ~26 lines.
Complete narrative walkthrough of fictional /dev 'Fix timeout in API' example covering all 15 steps. Not execution rules — tutorial documentation that does not guide agent execution behavior.

Aspirational checkboxes (100% requirement clarity before development, root cause identified, zero hardcoded values, QA passes within 3 iterations, etc.). These are not execution instructions; they repeat goals stated in agents/dev.md and agents/qa.md.
Cross-command reference block describing what /clean does with docs/dev/ artifacts. Not relevant to /dev orchestration execution.

codex_adjusted_false_positives.reason: Codex PROPOSED_FIX: section is a command-level artifact contract specifying task-id-stable filenames and JSON field matching — operationally relevant at the orchestrator layer, not pure agent-spec-only prose. Excluded from primary verbose count. Could be condensed to 3-4 lines but not a blocking finding.

Codex PROPOSED_FIX: role-boundary matrix defining what Dev enforces, what QA verifies, and what orchestrator owns. Substantive delegation contract. Excluded from primary verbose count. Could be condensed to 3 bullets.

findings.recommendation: Apply 'rules not stories' principle: remove Example End-to-End Workflow (42 lines), Success Metrics (15 lines), Integration with /clean (11 lines); condense Agent Development Use Cases (133 lines) by removing over-engineering examples and Why-rationale blocks while retaining scope/contract/ask-permission rules (~47 lines removable); condense Orchestrator Prompt Purity (55 lines) by removing incident narrative and worked examples, keeping only rule + enforcement pointer (~26 lines removable). Estimated total reduction: ~141 lines (17.0% -> ~7.6%). Reference: convert.md cleanup (commit 2d21631) reduced 113 lines (-22%) by removing similar verbose sections.

verbose_sections.notes: Summary section repeating commit message rules already stated in Phase 6 (lines 230-314). Below 50-line minimum threshold; advisory only.

advisory_only.recommendation: Advisory: merge into Phase 6 body or remove the duplicate summary section. Below minimum threshold for a formal finding.

no_findings.reason: All sections are operational rules. No verbose section patterns (Philosophy, Overview, Quality Standards, Safety Features, Helper Scripts) detected. All sections are below 50-line threshold individually.
Dense procedural contract throughout. No Philosophy, Overview, Quality Standards, or Safety Features sections detected. Verbose lines below threshold.

All sections are execution workflow steps or security procedure documentation. Privilege guard compatibility note (lines 256-276) is substantive security procedure — not a verbosity violation. Verbose lines below 50-line threshold.
Python source file. Prompt verbosity inspection applies only to Markdown documentation files.
Python source file. Prompt verbosity inspection applies only to Markdown documentation files.
Python source file. Prompt verbosity inspection applies only to Markdown documentation files.

summary.estimated_reduction: ~141 lines (-9.4%) if all recommendations applied to commands/dev.md

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed single-file finding on commands/dev.md and found no violations in the other 7 changed files. Key corrections applied: (1) severity corrected to critical (verbose_lines 256 >= 200 threshold); (2) JSON Storage Policy reclassified as false positive / advisory (command-level artifact contract); (3) Quality Standards Enforcement reclassified as false positive / advisory (role-boundary matrix); (4) Agent Development Use Cases finding nuanced — section kept in findings but recommendation now distinguishes removable example blocks from retainable procedural rules; (5) Orchestrator Prompt Purity (55 lines) added as a primary verbose section per codex's missed-verbosity flag.

findings.issue: Severity was major in initial draft but verbose_lines (256) meets critical threshold (>= 200)
findings.proposed_fix: Change severity from major to critical
findings.rationale: Correct per threshold table. Draft used the wrong severity branch.

findings.issue: Minor line count correction: Example End-to-End Workflow ends at 1435 not 1434; Success Metrics ends at 1450 not 1449
False positive: JSON Storage Policy (lines 1208-1226, 19 lines) is a command-level artifact contract with functional content
findings.proposed_fix: Exclude from primary verbose count; keep as advisory
findings.rationale: The section specifies artifact naming conventions, task-id contract, and retention policy that are operationally relevant. Reclassified as advisory.

findings.issue: False positive: Quality Standards Enforcement (lines 1238-1260, 23 lines) is a role-boundary delegation matrix
findings.proposed_fix: Exclude from primary verbose count; keep as advisory
findings.rationale: Delineates dev vs QA vs orchestrator enforcement domains — substantive delegation contract. Reclassified as advisory.

findings.issue: Partial false positive: Agent Development Use Cases (133 lines) contains real procedural rules; only example/rationale blocks are verbose
findings.proposed_fix: Retain section in findings but narrow recommendation to only removable sub-blocks; retain scope/contract/ask-permission/todo-requirement rules
findings.rationale: Section still meets critical-level threshold (133 lines). Recommendation updated to distinguish removable (~47 lines) vs retainable content within the section.

findings.issue: Missed verbose section: Orchestrator Prompt Purity (lines 1451-1505, 55 lines) contains incident narrative and worked examples
findings.proposed_fix: Add as a primary verbose section; keep rule + enforcement pointer, remove incident backstory and worked examples
findings.rationale: The rule statement is substantive but the origin narrative and two worked examples are story, not execution rules. Added to primary verbose sections.

findings.issue: OBSERVATION_ONLY: other changed files (CLAUDE.md, changelog-analyst.md, close.md, commit.md, py files) have no critical verbosity
findings.rationale: Consistent with inspector's no-violation finding for these files. Changelog-analyst.md has a minor advisory finding below threshold.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Severity corrected to critical; two false positives moved to advisory; Orchestrator Prompt Purity added as primary finding; Agent Development Use Cases recommendation nuanced. Net primary verbose line count: 256 lines (critical).
